title: Upload Medical Records with OCR Extraction

requirements.text: Add a backend endpoint that accepts an uploaded file (PDF or image encoded as binary/base64), stores the raw file data per principal, and returns a stable reference ID for the uploaded asset.

quotes: I need an option to upload record which will be in pdf or image

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes an `uploadFile` endpoint accepting file bytes and a MIME type (image/png, image/jpeg, application/pdf).
Uploaded file is stored per caller principal alongside a unique record ID and upload timestamp.
Endpoint returns the assigned file reference ID on success.

requirements.text: Add a backend OCR processing endpoint that takes a stored file reference ID, performs text extraction on the file content, and returns structured fields: detected date, test names, and test result values parsed from the extracted text.

quotes: it needs to be ocr and dates and test and test results needs to be captured

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes an `extractRecordFromFile` endpoint that accepts a file reference ID.
The endpoint performs OCR-style text parsing on the stored file bytes to extract date strings, test name tokens, and numeric result values.
Returns a structured response containing: detected date (ISO string or null), an array of { testName: Text, value: Text } pairs, and a raw extracted text string for user review.
Only the owning principal can trigger extraction on their own uploaded files.

requirements.text: Add an 'Upload Record' option to the Add Record page UI. The upload flow must allow the user to select or drag-and-drop a PDF or image file, display a preview or filename confirmation, trigger OCR extraction on the uploaded file, and pre-populate the medical record form fields (date, category, and metric values) with the extracted data for user review and correction before submission.

quotes: I need an option to upload record which will be in pdf or image and it needs to be ocr and dates and test and test results needs to be captured

acceptanceCriteria: The Add Record page shows a clearly labelled 'Upload Record' tab or toggle alongside the existing manual entry form.
The upload area accepts PDF and image files (image/png, image/jpeg, application/pdf) via file picker or drag-and-drop.
After a file is selected, a loading/processing state is shown while OCR extraction runs.
On extraction success, the date field, category selector, and relevant metric fields are pre-populated with detected values.
A read-only 'Extracted Text' expandable section shows the raw OCR output so the user can verify accuracy.
The user can edit any pre-populated field before submitting the record normally.
If OCR extraction returns no usable data, a clear error/warning message is shown and the form remains editable.
File size is validated client-side; files larger than 5 MB show a user-friendly error.

requirements.text: Add a `listUploadedFiles` backend endpoint that returns metadata (file ID, upload timestamp, MIME type) for all files uploaded by the calling principal, and a `deleteUploadedFile` endpoint to remove a file by ID.

quotes: I need an option to upload record which will be in pdf or image

acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated principals can list their own uploaded file metadata.
Authenticated principals can delete their own uploaded files by ID.
Principals cannot access or delete files belonging to other principals.

constraints: All file storage and OCR logic must remain within the single Motoko actor (no external services or microservices).
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
The existing manual medical record entry form must remain fully functional and unaltered.
File uploads must be scoped per caller principal — users can only access their own uploaded files.
nonGoals: Automatic submission of extracted records without user review and confirmation.
Integration with any external OCR API or third-party cloud service.
Storing rendered PDF page previews or image thumbnails beyond what is needed for OCR.
Support for file formats other than PDF, PNG, and JPEG.
Batch upload of multiple files simultaneously.
